---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
mu <- 0
sigma <- 0
```


```{r}
x <- seq(from = mu - 4*sigma, to = mu +4*sigma, length.out = 1000 )
```

```{r}
y <- dnorm(x, mean = mu, sd = sigma)
```


```{r}
plot (
  x, y, type = "l",
  main = "Normal PDF: M(0,1)",
  xlab = "x (value)", 
  ylab = "density f(x)",
  ylim = (0,35)
)
```

```{r}
# vertical line
abline(v =mu, lty =2)
```


# make fn and get values of y

# at given x, calculating the values



```{r}
2.# changing the mean (mu) shifts the distribution
#Keep sd fixed so only, the location schanges
# here: take the smallest mean and largest nean and extend +/- sd a

x2 <- seq(from = min(mus) - 4 *sig, to = max(mus) + 4*sig, length.out =1000)
```

```{r}
 plot(
   x2, dnorm(x2, mean = mus[1],sd sig),
   type ="l",
   main = "changing the mean of variance"
 )
```

```{r}
# add other curves on the same plot
lines(x2,dnorm(x2, mean = mus[2],sd =sig), lty =2)
lines(x2,dnorm(x2, mean = mus[2],sd =sig), lty =3)
```
